The '''stalk-eyed species''' was a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that lived throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].

==Biology and appearance==
[[File:Stalk_in_cantina.png|thumb|left|upright|Two members in a cantina]]
This alien species was reptilian in appearance, and appeared to average around 0.6 meters. They had distinctive stalk-mounted eyes, which appeared to be flexible and capable of looking in two directions at once. They also had a large underbite, with a number of small sharp teeth jutting up from their lower jaw.<ref name="WBC" />

Most members of the species had four arms, although it appears some may have had only two.<ref name="OpEl">[[Operation: Elrood (book)|''Operation: Elrood'']]</ref> Each of their arms ended in three-fingered hands with an opposable thumb, and their feet similarly had three toes.<ref name="WBC" />

==Presence in the galaxy==
[[File:Alien_on_Tresidiss.jpg|thumb|right|Two members on Tresidiss]]
Two members of this species worked with the [[Hethas]] criminal mastermind [[Gornt Seron]], who was based on the planet [[Korbin]]. These two had direct contact with Seron, and appeared to be on reasonably familiar terms with him.<ref name="WBC" /> Some other members of the species lived on the planet [[Tresidiss]], notorious for its gambling establishments run by [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] criminal syndicates, in which they appeared to be involved.<ref name="GFT">''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]''</ref>

Other members of the species also lived on [[Derilyn]] in the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]].<ref name="OpEl" />

==Behind the scenes==
This species has appeared as background individuals in at least five illustrations by [[Mike Vilardi]] for the [[West End Games]] [[roleplaying game]] sourcebooks.<ref name="WBC" /><ref name="OpEl" /><ref name="GFT" /><ref name="SWRPG2E">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition]]</ref><ref name="GG11">''[[Galaxy Guide 11: Criminal Organizations]]''</ref> They appear similar in form to the [[Glurrg]]s of ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''.<ref name="GB">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref>

In [[2011]], Vilardi was prompted by a fan to name this species, and he dubbed them "'''seidaots'''" (pronounced "say-day-ahts"), or "'''seidaotians'''."<ref name="seidaots">{{DeviantArt|username=caesar120|url=comments/1/207549426/2222739439|name=Mike Vilardi|text=Comment on 'Checkpoint' by caesar120|quote=Name them, eh? Hmmm...ok. I hereby decree that these creatures are to be known (in true Lucas fashion)as seidaots (seidaotians). Pronounced say-day-ahts. You heard it here first.}}</ref> In [[2025]], Keeper of the Holocron [[Leland Chee]] stated that these names were never made official and that as of [[December 1]], [[2025]], the species still did not have a name within the [[Holocron continuity database]]. Chee also stated that he also considered the possibility that, due to their differing numbers of limbs, they were actually two separate species altogether.<ref name="LeelandChee">{{WookDiscord|discordname=.holocronkeeper|name=Leland Chee|date=2025-12-01|messagelink=https://discord.com/channels/397848917036761090/492457166771322881/1445150306559201521|quote=Those aren't official names. We have one for Fud Sang but not the WEG species. It having 2-armed and 4-armed versions makes me wonder if it's not two separate species. Also reminds me of Noti and Zilkins. Looks like I need a new entry  reflecting all our short, eye-stalk species.|archivefile=File:UnofficialSpeciesNames-LelandChee.png}} {{C|In response to: "Also I guess while I have you, and if you don't mind, would you entertain one other two-pronged question I've been sitting on for a while? 😅 We have a couple of cases of official folks talking about names for species that have just never been reiterated anywhere else, namely Tim Veekhoven calling Fud Sang's species the "Sanhueli" and Mike Vilardi calling this species that cropped up a fair bit across some of the old RPG materials the "Seidaots/Seidaotians," are either of these names official in any capacity? Or just something these folks came up with on their own?"}}</ref>
